item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 4.0
Title: Modern house in Sherwood, Queensland.jpg
Author: Kgbo
Release year: 2017

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe they left for a vacation or clubbed all night. Other possibilities also exist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it only effects vacant houses but was discovered when residents reported their houses had grown an extension????

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for pointing it out, it’s done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The link to 474 seems exceedingly random.
How would dream radio pills relate to house parasites?

I can only presume the slot used to be something else and has been replaced since this was first posted.

In any case, the 474 should probably be taken out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When this process is completed, the SCP-1452 instance(s) will become dormant until the house becomes occupied.

I don't understand why they won't let the instance they have integrate into its containment cell if it won't reproduce if it's unoccupied. Why don't they just build it a house somewhere outside on the grounds of some facility and let it sit there indefinitely? If at any time it spontaneously reproduces (while it's unoccupied) wouldn't it be easy enough to throw up a shed for it and burn down the mature instance?

Edited because I can't markdown good.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

With three crosslinks already in place, this article is already done from Wikiwalk standards. That being said, as a primary Psi-7 article, it wouldn't hurt to also link to the new Psi-7 hub, given that links out to yet more articles. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Uncontained instances of SCP-1452 are to be captured by MTF-ψ-7 "Home Improvement." Reports of SCP-1452 activity may also be related to SCP-474 or SCP-1992.

To this:

Uncontained instances of SCP-1452 are to be captured by MTF-ψ-7 "Home Improvement." Reports of SCP-1452 activity may also be related to SCP-474 or SCP-1992.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Demolishing a poor neighborhood is probably fairly low on the list of morally questionable things the Foundation has done to maintain containment, but damn if it doesn't still get a reaction from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the nature instances didn't fill with a material, this seems like it would actually be a viable way of getting houses.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It wrecks houses. What else do you think it would need to do?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do they do anything bad to people? i want to like it but it seems to be missing something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept and the effect. Quirky & interesting. +1'ed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love SCPs that give inanimate objects the habits of living creatures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this a lot, although the sentence "Mature instances of SCP-1452 will continuously produce immature SCP-1452's" doesn't sit right with me. It would sound better if you rewrote it as "Mature instances of SCP-1452 will continuously produce additional immature instances of SCP-1452," IMO. Still, good read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unrelated to the matter at hand, but it seems that I must now fight you for the title of Friendleader. Nothing personal.

On-topic: I dig this one. Weird and nifty. +1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would assume that most folks would have vacated around the time the original building was about to (or in the middle of) collapsing. Especially since nothing states that the parasite-extensions are following any specific room plan or the like, so it would be difficult for the family to continue living in a pile of rubble attached to 3 reading rooms, a sun porch (that's on the bottom of two floors and in the middle of the building) and a half-bath with only a toilet and a sink.

Oh, and a linen closet. For some reason, no matter how shoddy or tiny a place may be, there is ALWAYS a linen closet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do the mature instances fill up with solid material when there are occupants? Are they consumed in the process?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not a phineas and ferb reference, I was in a town called danville located in PA when I wrote it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Overall, not bad, though there's a few grammar errors, and the apparent Phineas & Ferb reference was a bit jarring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So THAT'S what's happening to my house… Gosh-darned extension! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be honest, this feels way too similar to SCP-836. Yes I did write that one, but still.
-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I guess Dr. Doofenshmirtz forgot to shut down his house-takeover-inator.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciated that, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Danville, Pennsylvania

Phineas? Ferb?

Other than that…odd reference, I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Attempting to enter the SCP-1452 instance prior to integration will show that is in constructed out of solid concrete or wood."
I believe you need to take out the 'in' in this sentence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since they appear to be very similar in appearance to shipping containers, they could easily pass for a shipping container mother-in-law suite or shed or other such detached building.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They don't. They're usually just close enough to another house when they're created that they can get there without being spotted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As large and as slow as these things are, how the hell do they go unnoticed by the general public?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At least 25% of my upvote is because nothing was redacted or black-boxed. Just so you know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: House parasites? Nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Coating a floor with glass.
Much better said that the floor is to be lined with (some thickness of) glass.

Otherwise, well done.
Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting, effect. Not much else to say.
